---
title: "Talk Title"
subtitle: "Subtitle / Conference Name"
author: "Your Name · Collaborators: A, B, C"
format:
  revealjs:
    theme: simple
    width: 1600
    height: 900
    slide-number: false
    chalkboard: false
    controls: false
    margin: 0
    fig-cap-location: bottom
    transition: none
    incremental: false
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        <script src="talk-template.js"></script>
execute:
  echo: false
  warning: false
  message: false
title-slide-attributes:
  data-background-image: "background.svg"
  data-background-size: cover
  data-background-position: center
---

## Overview {data-section="Introduction"}

::: incremental
- **Section tabs** are highlighted automatically based on `data-section="..."`
- The footer shows your name, date, and slide number
- Edit `CFG` at the top of `talk-template.js` to customise everything
:::

::: {.fragment style="border: 2px solid #333; padding: 0.7em; border-radius: 8px;"}
Sections defined in `talk-template.js`:  `Introduction` · `Methods` · `Results` · `Conclusion`
:::

## Two-column layout {data-section="Introduction"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="55%"}

Use `::: {.columns}` for side-by-side content.

::: incremental
- First point
- Second point
- Third point
:::

:::
::: {.column width="45%"}

Equations work out of the box:

$$\frac{\partial \mathbf{B}}{\partial t} = \nabla \times (\mathbf{u} \times \mathbf{B})$$

$$E(k) \propto k^{-5/3}$$

:::
:::

## Slide with a figure {data-section="Methods"}

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(8, 4))
k = np.logspace(0, 2, 200)
ax.loglog(k, k**(-5/3), lw=2.5, label=r"$k^{-5/3}$ (Kolmogorov)")
ax.loglog(k, 0.3 * k**(-2),  lw=2.5, ls="--", label=r"$k^{-2}$")
ax.set_xlabel("Wavenumber $k$", fontsize=14)
ax.set_ylabel("Energy $E(k)$", fontsize=14)
ax.legend(fontsize=13)
ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
plt.tight_layout()
```

## Bare-background slide {background-image="background.svg" background-size="cover" data-section="Results"}

<div style="
  font-size: 1.1em;
  background: rgba(255,255,255,0.85);
  padding: 1em 1.5em;
  border-radius: 10px;
  display: inline-block;
  margin-top: 2em;
">

Slides with `background-image:` skip the folder wrapper — useful for full-bleed visuals or dramatic section breaks.

</div>

## Results {data-section="Results"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}

```{python}
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import numpy as np

rng = np.random.default_rng(42)
x = np.linspace(0, 10, 300)
y = np.sin(x) + 0.15 * rng.standard_normal(300)

fig, ax = plt.subplots(figsize=(6, 4))
ax.plot(x, y, lw=1.5, alpha=0.8)
ax.plot(x, np.sin(x), lw=2, color="C1", label="truth")
ax.legend(fontsize=12)
ax.spines["top"].set_visible(False)
ax.spines["right"].set_visible(False)
plt.tight_layout()
```

:::
::: {.column width="50%"}

::: incremental
- Key finding one
- Key finding two
:::

::: {.fragment style="border: 2px solid black; padding: 0.8em; border-radius: 8px; margin-top: 1em;"}
$\rightarrow$ **Main takeaway**
:::

:::
:::

## Conclusion {data-section="Conclusion"}

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="60%"}

::: incremental
- Summary point one
- Summary point two
- Summary point three
:::

::: {.fragment style="text-align: center; margin-top: 2em;"}
**Thank you!**

Questions?
:::

:::
::: {.column width="40%"}

Replace with your paper QR code or key figure.

:::
:::
